  "english": {
    "IsA": [
      "It is an icon.",
      "It is one of the most venerated Christian icons.",
      "It is a fine and early example of the iconography of the Haghiosoritissa type.",
      "It is a miraculous image."
    ],
    "HasA": [
      "It has a connection with a certain type of Marian imagery.",
      "It has a wooden board backing."
    ],
    "HasFeatureOf": [
      "It is characterized by the dimness and softness of the colors.",
      "It is characterized by the asymmetric shape of the lengthened face.",
      "It is characterized by eyes with an intense look.",
      "It is characterized by a wide and thin mouth.",
      "It is characterized by the search of a dramatic effect on the part of the artist.",
      "It is characterized by the poses of the Virgin in the common image of the Deesis."
    ],
    "UsedFor": [
      "It is used for attracting many Christian pilgrims.",
      "It is used for invoking the mercy of Christ as judge.",
      "It is used for representing the theme of the prayer of Intercession.",
      "It is used for devotion.",
      "It is used for displaying the Virgin without the Child."
    ],
    "LocatedIn": [
      "It is located in the Basilica of the Saints Bonifacio and Alexis.",
      "It is located on the Aventine Hill in Rome, Italy.",
      "It is located in the Chapel of the Most Holy Sacrament.",
      "It is located on the right side of the south transept of the basilica.",
      "It is located above the altar of this Chapel."
    ],
    "BuiltIn": [
      "unknown"
    ],

    ],

    "ConsistsOf": [

      "It consists of a wooden board backing.",
      "It consists of figures of the Virgin and Saint John the Evangelist flanking Christ."
    ],
    "PartOf": [
      "It is part of the Basilica of the Saints Bonifacio and Alexis.",
      "It is part of the Chapel of the Most Holy Sacrament."
    ],
    "MemberOf": [
      "unknown"
    ],
    "BelongsTo": [
      "It belongs to the Basilica of the Saints Bonifacio and Alexis."
    ],
    "KnownFor": [
      "It is known for being miraculous.",
      "It is known for attracting many Christian pilgrims.",
      "It is known as «Madonna di Edessa».",
      "It is known as «Madonna Avvocata».",
      "It is known as «Madonna dell'Intercessione»."
    ],
    "AssociatedWith": [
      "It is associated with the Haghiosoritissa type iconography.",
      "It is associated with the Deesis.",
      "It is associated with St Luke the Evangelist.",
      "It is associated with St. Alexius.",
      "It is associated with the legend about St. Alexius.",
      "It is associated with Constantinople.",
      "It is associated with Rome.",
      "It is associated with Pope Innocent X.",
      "It is associated with the Somascan Fathers.",
      "It is associated with the High Institute for Conservation and Restoration."
    ],
    "NamedAfter": [
      "It is named after St. Alexis.",
      "It is named after Intercession."
    ],
    "OccurredIn": [
      "It occurred in the 12th and 13th centuries.",
      "It occurred in 1952.",
      "It occurred in 2014-2015."
    ],

      "It was held in Edessa church of the Blessed Virgin.",
      "It was held in Rome."
    ],

  "japanese": {
    "IsA": [
      "それはイコンです。",
      "それは最も崇敬されているキリスト教のイコンの一つです。",
      "それはハギオソリチッサ様式の図像の初期の優れた例です。",
      "それは奇跡的なイメージです。"
    ],
    "HasA": [
      "それは特定の聖母マリアの図像との関連を持っています。",
      "それは木製の板の裏打ちを持っています。"
    ],
    "HasFeatureOf": [
      "それは色彩の薄暗さと柔らかさによって特徴づけられます。",
      "それは細長い顔の非対称な形状によって特徴づけられます。",
      "それは強烈な視線の目によって特徴づけられます。",
      "それは広く薄い口によって特徴づけられます。",
      "それは芸術家による劇的な効果の追求によって特徴づけられます。",
      "それはデエシス（Deesis）の一般的なイメージにおける聖母の姿勢によって特徴づけられます。"
    ],
    "UsedFor": [
      "それは多くのキリスト教の巡礼者を引き付けるために使用されます。",
      "それはキリストの裁き人としての慈悲を呼び起こすために使用されます。",
      "それは執り成しの祈りのテーマを表すために使用されます。",
      "それは崇敬のために使用されます。",
      "それは子供のいない聖母を表すために使用されます。"
    ],
    "LocatedIn": [
      "それは聖ボニファティウスとアレクシウスのバシリカにあります。",
      "それはローマのアヴェンティーノの丘にあります。",
      "それは至聖聖体礼拝堂にあります。",
      "それはバシリカの南翼廊の右側にあります。",
      "それはこの礼拝堂の祭壇の上にあります。"
    ],
    "BuiltIn": [
      "unknown"
    ],
    "BuiltBy": [
      "unknown"
    ],
    "OriginatedIn": [
      "それはビザンティウムで生まれました。",
      "それは東方で生まれました。"
    ],
    "FoundedBy": [
      "unknown"
    ],
    "FoundedIn": [
      "unknown"
    ],
    "CreatedBy": [
      "それは伝承によれば聖ルカ福音記者によって創造されました。",
      "それは芸術家によって創造されました。"
    ],
    "CreatedIn": [
      "それはビザンティウムで創造されました。",
      "それは12世紀から13世紀に創造されました。"
    ],
    "DevelopedBy": [
      "unknown"
    ],
    "DevelopedIn": [

    ],
    "DiscoveredBy": [
      "unknown"
    ],

      "unknown"

      "unknown"
    ],
    "ManufacturedBy": [
      "unknown"
    ],
    "ProducedBy": [
      "それはテンペラとキャンバスで制作されました。"
    ],
    "CausedBy": [
      "unknown"
    ],
    "Affects": [
      "unknown"
    ],
    "ConsistsOf": [
      "それはテンペラとキャンバスで構成されています。",
      "それは木製の板の裏打ちで構成されています。",
      "それはキリストを挟む聖母と福音記者聖ヨハネの姿で構成されています。"
    ],
    "PartOf": [
      "それは聖ボニファティウスとアレクシウスのバシリカの一部です。",
      "それは至聖聖体礼拝堂の一部です。"
    ],
    "MemberOf": [
      "unknown"
    ],
    "BelongsTo": [
      "それは聖ボニファティウスとアレクシウスのバシリカに属しています。"
    ],
    "KnownFor": [
      "それは奇跡的であることで知られています。",
      "それは多くのキリスト教の巡礼者を引き付けることで知られています。",
      "それは「エデッサの聖母」として知られています。",
      "それは「弁護者（アヴォッカータ）の聖母」として知られています。",
      "それは「執り成しの聖母」として知られています。"
    ],
    "AssociatedWith": [
      "それはハギオソリチッサ様式の図像と関連があります。",
      "それはデエシス（Deesis）と関連があります。",
      "それは聖ルカ福音記者と関連があります。",
      "それは聖アレクシウスと関連があります。",
      "それは聖アレクシウスに関する伝説と関連があります。",
      "それはコンスタンティノープルと関連があります。",
      "それはローマと関連があります。",
      "それは教皇インノケンティウス10世と関連があります。",
      "それはソマスカ会士と関連があります。",
      "それは高等保存修復研究所と関連があります。"
    ],
    "NamedAfter": [
      "それは聖アレクシウスにちなんで名付けられました。",
      "それは執り成しにちなんで名付けられました。"
    ],
    "OccurredIn": [
      "それは12世紀から13世紀に起こりました。",
      "それは1952年に起こりました。",
      "それは2014年から2015年に起こりました。"
    ],
    "HeldIn": [
      "それはエデッサの聖母教会に保管されていました。",
      "それはローマに保管されていました。"
    ],

      "unknown"
    ],
    "About": [
      "それは聖母マリアについてです。",
      "それは執り成しの祈りについてです。",
      "それはキリストの裁き人としての慈悲についてです。",
      "それは聖アレクシウスの生涯についてです。"
    ],
    "InfluencedBy": [
      "それはビザンティン様式の影響を受けました。",
      "それはデエシス（Deesis）の一般的なイメージの影響を受けました。"
    ],
    "Influenced": [
      "unknown"
    ]
  }

IsA: It is one of the most venerated Christian icons.
It is a fine and early example of the iconography of the Haghiosoritissa type.
HasA: It has a connection with a certain type of Marian imagery.
It has a wooden board backing.
HasFeatureOf: It is characterized by the dimness and softness of the colors.
It is characterized by the asymmetric shape of the lengthened face.
It is characterized by eyes with an intense look.
It is characterized by a wide and thin mouth.
It is characterized by the search of a dramatic effect on the part of the artist.
It is characterized by the poses of the Virgin in the common image of the Deesis.
UsedFor: It is used for attracting many Christian pilgrims.
It is used for invoking the mercy of Christ as judge.
It is used for representing the theme of the prayer of Intercession.
It is used for displaying the Virgin without the Child.
LocatedIn: It is located in the Basilica of the Saints Bonifacio and Alexis.
It is located on the Aventine Hill in Rome, Italy.
It is located in the Chapel of the Most Holy Sacrament.
It is located on the right side of the south transept of the basilica.
It is located above the altar of this Chapel.
CreatedBy: It was created by St Luke the Evangelist according to tradition.
It was created by an artist.
CreatedIn: It was created in the 12th and the 13th century.
ProducedBy: It was produced with tempera on canvas.
ConsistsOf: It consists of tempera on canvas.
It consists of a wooden board backing.
It consists of figures of the Virgin and Saint John the Evangelist flanking Christ.
PartOf: It is part of the Basilica of the Saints Bonifacio and Alexis.
It is part of the Chapel of the Most Holy Sacrament.
BelongsTo: It belongs to the Basilica of the Saints Bonifacio and Alexis.
KnownFor: It is known for being miraculous.
It is known for attracting many Christian pilgrims.
It is known as «Madonna di Edessa».
AssociatedWith: It is associated with the Haghiosoritissa type iconography.
It is associated with the Deesis.
It is associated with St Luke the Evangelist.
It is associated with St. Alexius.
It is associated with the legend about St. Alexius.
It is associated with Constantinople.
It is associated with Rome.
It is associated with Pope Innocent X.
It is associated with the Somascan Fathers.
It is associated with the High Institute for Conservation and Restoration.
NamedAfter: It is named after St. Alexis.
OccurredIn: It occurred in the 12th and 13th centuries.
HeldIn: It was held in Edessa church of the Blessed Virgin.
About: It is about the Blessed Virgin.
It is about the prayer of Intercession.
It is about the mercy of Christ as judge.
It is about the life of St. Alexius.
InfluencedBy: It was influenced by Byzantine patterns.
It was influenced by the common image of the Deesis.
